translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Paul, [a] called apostle of Jesus Christ, byGod's will, and Sosthenes the brother,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to the assembly ofGod which is in Corinth, to [those] sanctified in Christ Jesus, called saints, with all that in every place call on the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, both theirs and ours:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Grace to you and peace fromGod our Father, and [the] Lord Jesus Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I thank myGod always about you, in respect of the grace ofGod given to you in Christ Jesus;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that in everything ye have been enriched in him, in all word [of doctrine], and all knowledge,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (according as the testimony of the Christ has been confirmed in you,)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so that ye come short in no gift, awaiting the revelation of our Lord Jesus Christ;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who shall also confirm you to [the] end, unimpeachable in the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God [is] faithful, by whom ye have been called into [the] fellowship of his Son Jesus Christ our Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now I exhort you, brethren, by the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that ye all say the same thing, and that there be not among you divisions; but that ye be perfectly united in the same mind and in the same opinion.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For it has been shewn to me concerning you, my brethren, by those of [the house of] Chloe, that there are strifes among you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I speak of this, that each of you says, I am of Paul, and I of Apollos, and I of Cephas, and I of Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is the Christ divided? has Paul been crucified for you? or have ye been baptised unto the name of Paul?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I thankGod that I have baptised none of you, unless Crispus and Gaius,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that no one may say that I have baptised unto my own name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yes, I baptised also the house of Stephanas; for the rest I know not if I have baptised any other.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Christ has not sent me to baptise, but to preach glad tidings; not in wisdom of word, that the cross of the Christ may not be made vain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the word of the cross is to them that perish foolishness, but to us that are saved it isGod's power.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For it is written, I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and set aside the understanding of the understanding ones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Where [is the] wise? where scribe? where disputer of this world? has notGod made foolish the wisdom of the world?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For since, in the wisdom ofGod, the world by wisdom has not knownGod,God has been pleased by the foolishness of the preaching to save those that believe.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Since Jews indeed ask for signs, and Greeks seek wisdom;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but we preach Christ crucified, to Jews an offence, and to nations foolishness;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but to those that [are] called, both Jews and Greeks, ChristGod's power andGod's wisdom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because the foolishness ofGod is wiser than men, and the weakness ofGod is stronger than men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For consider your calling, brethren, that [there are] not many wise according to flesh, not many powerful, not many high-born.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ButGod has chosen the foolish things of the world, that he may put to shame the wise; andGod has chosen the weak things of the world, that he may put to shame the strong things;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the ignoble things of the world, and the despised, hasGod chosen, [and] things that are not, that he may annul the things that are;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so that no flesh should boast beforeGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But of him are ye in Christ Jesus, who has been made to us wisdom fromGod, and righteousness, and holiness, and redemption;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that according as it is written, He that boasts, let him boast in [the] Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I, when I came to you, brethren, came not in excellency of word, or wisdom, announcing to you the testimony ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I did not judge [it well] to know anything among you save Jesus Christ, and him crucified.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I was with you in weakness and in fear and in much trembling;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and my word and my preaching, not in persuasive words of wisdom, but in demonstration of [the] Spirit and of power;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that your faith might not stand in men's wisdom, but inGod's power.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But we speak wisdom among the perfect; but wisdom not of this world, nor of the rulers of this world, who come to nought.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But we speakGod's wisdom in [a] mystery, that hidden [wisdom] whichGod had predetermined before the ages for our glory:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: which none of the princes of this age knew, (for had they known, they would not have crucified the Lord of glory;)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but according as it is written, Things which eye has not seen, and ear not heard, and which have not come into man's heart, whichGod has prepared for them that love him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: butGod has revealed to us by [his] Spirit; for the Spirit searches all things, even the depths ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For who of men hath known the things of a man except the spirit of the man which is in him? thus also the things ofGod knows no one except the Spirit ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But we have received, not the spirit of the world, but the Spirit which [is] ofGod, that we may know the things which have been freely given to us ofGod:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: which also we speak, not in words taught by human wisdom, but in those taught by the Spirit, communicating spiritual [things] by spiritual [means].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But [the] natural man does not receive the things of the Spirit ofGod, for they are folly to him; and he cannot know [them] because they are spiritually discerned;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but the spiritual discerns all things, and he is discerned of no one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For who has known the mind of [the] Lord, who shall instruct him? But we have the mind of Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I, brethren, have not been able to speak to you as to spiritual, but as to fleshly; as to babes in Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have given you milk to drink, not meat, for ye have not yet been able, nor indeed are ye yet able;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for ye are yet carnal. For whereas [there are] among you emulation and strife, are ye not carnal, and walk according to man?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For when one says, I am of Paul, and another, I of Apollos, are ye not men?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who then is Apollos, and who Paul? Ministering servants, through whom ye have believed, and as the Lord has given to each.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have planted; Apollos watered; butGod has given the increase.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So that neither the planter is anything, nor the waterer; butGod the giver of the increase.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the planter and the waterer are one; but each shall receive his own reward according to his own labour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we areGod's fellow-workmen; ye areGod's husbandry,God's building.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: According to the grace ofGod which has been given to me, as a wise architect, I have laid the foundation, but another builds upon it. But let each see how he builds upon it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For other foundation can no man lay besides that which [is] laid, which is Jesus Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now if any one build upon [this] foundation, gold, silver, precious stones, wood, grass, straw,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the work of each shall be made manifest; for the day shall declare [it], because it is revealed in fire; and the fire shall try the work of each what it is.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If the work of any one which he has built upon [the foundation] shall abide, he shall receive a reward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If the work of any one shall be consumed, he shall suffer loss, but he shall be saved, but so as through [the] fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do ye not know that ye are [the] temple ofGod, and [that] the Spirit ofGod dwells in you?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If any one corrupt the temple ofGod, him shallGod destroy; for the temple ofGod is holy, and such are ye.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let no one deceive himself: if any one thinks himself to be wise among you in this world, let him become foolish, that he may be wise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the wisdom of this world is foolishness withGod; for it is written, He who takes the wise in their craftiness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And again, [The] Lord knows the reasonings of the wise that they are vain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So that let no one boast in men; for all things are yours.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whether Paul, or Apollos, or Cephas, or [the] world, or life, or death, or things present, or things coming, all are yours;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ye [are] Christ's, and Christ [is]God's.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let a man so account of us as servants of Christ, and stewards of [the] mysteries ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Here, further, it is sought in stewards, that a man be found faithful.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But for me it is the very smallest matter that I be examined of you or of man's day. Nor do I even examine myself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I am conscious of nothing in myself; but I am not justified by this: but he that examines me is the Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So that do not judge anything before [the] time, until the Lord shall come, who shall also both bring to light the hidden things of darkness, and shall make manifest the counsels of hearts; and then shall each have [his] praise fromGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now these things, brethren, I have transferred, in their application, to myself and Apollos, for your sakes, that ye may learn in us the [lesson of] not [letting your thoughts go] above what is written, that ye may not be puffed up one for [such a] one against another.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For who makes thee to differ? and what hast thou which thou hast not received? but if also thou hast received, why boastest thou as not receiving?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Already ye are filled; already ye have been enriched; ye have reigned without us; and I would that ye reigned, that we also might reign with you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I think thatGod has set us the apostles for the last, as appointed to death. For we have become a spectacle to the world, both to angels and men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We [are] fools for Christ's sake, but ye prudent in Christ: we weak, but ye strong: ye glorious, but we in dishonour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To the present hour we both hunger and thirst, and are in nakedness, and buffeted, and wander without a home,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and labour, working with our own hands. Railed at, we bless; persecuted, we suffer [it];

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: insulted, we entreat: we are become as [the] offscouring of the world, [the] refuse of all, until now.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Not [as] chiding do I write these things to you, but as my beloved children I admonish [you].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if ye should have ten thousand instructors in Christ, yet not many fathers; for in Christ Jesus I have begotten you through the glad tidings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I entreat you therefore, be my imitators.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this reason I have sent to you Timotheus, who is my beloved and faithful child in [the] Lord, who shall put you in mind of my ways [as] they [are] in Christ, according as I teach everywhere in every assembly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But some have been puffed up, as if I were not coming to you;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but I will come quickly to you, if the Lord will; and I will know, not the word of those that are puffed up, but the power.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the kingdom ofGod [is] not in word, but in power.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What will ye? that I come to you with a rod; or in love, and [in] a spirit of meekness?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is universally reported [that there is] fornication among you, and such fornication as [is] not even among the nations, so that one should have his father's wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye are puffed up, and ye have not rather mourned, in order that he that has done this deed might be taken away out of the midst of you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I, [as] absent in body but present in spirit, have already judged as present,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [to deliver,] in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ (ye and my spirit being gathered together, with the power of our Lord Jesus Christ), him that has so wrought this:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to deliver him, [I say,] [being] such, to Satan for destruction of the flesh, that the spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Your boasting [is] not good. Do ye not know that a little leaven leavens the whole lump?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Purge out the old leaven, that ye may be a new lump, according as ye are unleavened. For also our passover, Christ, has been sacrificed;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so that let us celebrate the feast, not with old leaven, nor with leaven of malice and wickedness, but with unleavened [bread] of sincerity and truth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have written to you in the epistle not to mix with fornicators;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: not altogether with the fornicators of this world, or with the avaricious and rapacious, or idolaters, since [then] ye should go out of the world.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But now I have written to you, if any one called brother be fornicator, or avaricious, or idolater, or abusive, or a drunkard, or rapacious, not to mix with [him]; with such a one not even to eat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For what have I [to do] with judging those outside also? ye, do not ye judge them that are within?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But those withoutGod judges. Remove the wicked person from amongst yourselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Dare any one of you, having a matter against another, prosecute his suit before the unjust, and not before the saints?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do ye not then know that the saints shall judge the world? and if the world is judged by you, are ye unworthy of [the] smallest judgments?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do ye not know that we shall judge angels? and not then matters of this life?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If then ye have judgments as to things of this life, set those [to judge] who are little esteemed in the assembly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I speak to you [to put you] to shame. Thus there is not a wise person among you, not even one, who shall be able to decide between his brethren!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But brother prosecutes his suit with brother, and that before unbelievers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Already indeed then it is altogether a fault in you that ye have suits between yourselves. Why do ye not rather suffer wrong? why are ye not rather defrauded?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But ye do wrong, and defraud, and this [your] brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do ye not know that unrighteous [persons] shall not inherit [the] kingdom ofGod? Do not err: neither fornicators, nor idolaters, nor adulterers, nor those who make women of themselves, nor who abuse themselves with men,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: nor thieves, nor covetous, nor drunkards, nor abusive persons, nor [the] rapacious, shall inherit [the] kingdom ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these things were some of you; but ye have been washed, but ye have been sanctified, but ye have been justified in the name of the Lord Jesus, and by the Spirit of ourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All things are lawful to me, but all things do not profit; all things are lawful to me, but I will not be brought under the power of any.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Meats for the belly, and the belly for meats; butGod will bring to nothing both it and them: but the body [is] not for fornication, but for the Lord, and the Lord for the body.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod has both raised up the Lord, and will raise us up from among [the dead] by his power.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do ye not know that your bodies are members of Christ? Shall I then, taking the members of the Christ, make [them] members of a harlot? Far be the thought.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do ye not know that he [that is] joined to the harlot is one body? for the two, he says, shall be one flesh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he that [is] joined to the Lord is one Spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Flee fornication. Every sin which a man may practise is without the body, but he that commits fornication sins against his own body.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do ye not know that your body is [the] temple of the Holy Spirit which [is] in you, which ye have ofGod; and ye are not your own?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for ye have been bought with a price: glorify now thenGod in your body.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But concerning the things of which ye have written [to me]: [It is] good for a man not to touch a woman;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but on account of fornications, let each have his own wife, and each [woman] have her own husband.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the husband render her due to the wife, and in like manner the wife to the husband.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wife has not authority over her own body, but the husband: in like manner also the husband has not authority over his own body, but the wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Defraud not one another, unless, it may be, by consent for a time, that ye may devote yourselves to prayer, and again be together, that Satan tempt you not because of your incontinency.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But this I say, as consenting [to], not as commanding [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now I wish all men to be even as myself: but every one has his own gift ofGod: one man thus, and another thus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I say to the unmarried and to the widows, It is good for them that they remain even as I.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if they have not control over themselves, let them marry; for it is better to marry than to burn.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But to the married I enjoin, not I, but the Lord, Let not wife be separated from husband;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (but if also she shall have been separated, let her remain unmarried, or be reconciled to her husband;) and let not husband leave wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as to the rest, I say, not the Lord, If any brother have an unbelieving wife, and she consent to dwell with him, let him not leave her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a woman who has an unbelieving husband, and he consents to dwell with her, let her not leave [her] husband.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the unbelieving husband is sanctified in the wife, and the unbelieving wife is sanctified in the brother; since [otherwise] indeed your children are unclean, but now they are holy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if the unbeliever go away, let them go away; a brother or a sister is not bound in such [cases], butGod has called us in peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For what knowest thou, O wife, if thou shalt save thy husband? or what knowest thou, O husband, if thou shalt save thy wife?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: However, as the Lord has divided to each, asGod has called each, so let him walk; and thus I ordain in all the assemblies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Has any one been called circumcised? let him not become uncircumcised: has any one been called in uncircumcision? let him not be circumcised.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Circumcision is nothing, and uncircumcision is nothing; but keepingGod's commandments.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let each abide in that calling in which he has been called.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hast thou been called [being] a bondman, let it not concern thee; but and if thou canst become free, use [it] rather.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the bondman that is called in [the] Lord is the Lord's freedman; in like manner [also] the freeman being called is Christ's bondman.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye have been bought with a price; do not be the bondmen of men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let each, wherein he is called, brethren, therein abide withGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But concerning virgins, I have no commandment of [the] Lord; but I give my opinion, as having received mercy of [the] Lord to be faithful.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I think then that this is good, on account of the present necessity, that [it is] good for a man to remain so as he is.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Art thou bound to a wife? seek not to be loosed; art thou free from a wife? do not seek a wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if thou shouldest also marry, thou hast not sinned; and if the virgin marry, they have not sinned: but such shall have tribulation in the flesh; but I spare you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But this I say, brethren, the time is straitened. For the rest, that they who have wives, be as not having [any]:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they that weep, as not weeping; and they that rejoice, as not rejoicing; and they that buy, as not possessing;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they that use the world, as not disposing of it as their own; for the fashion of this world passes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I wish you to be without care. The unmarried cares for the things of the Lord, how he shall please the Lord;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but he that has married cares for the things of the world, how he shall please his wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is a difference between the wife and the virgin. The unmarried cares for the things of the Lord, that she may be holy both in body and spirit; but she that has married cares for the things of the world, how she shall please her husband.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I say this for your own profit; not that I may set a snare before you, but for what [is] seemly, and waiting on the Lord without distraction.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if any one think that he behaves unseemly to his virginity, if he be beyond the flower of his age, and so it must be, let him do what he will, he does not sin: let them marry.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he who stands firm in his heart, having no need, but has authority over his own will, and has judged this in his heart to keep his own virginity, he does well.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So that he that marries himself does well; and he that does not marry does better.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A wife is bound for whatever time her husband lives; but if the husband be fallen asleep, she is free to be married to whom she will, only in [the] Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But she is happier if she so remain, according to my judgment; but I think that I also haveGod's Spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But concerning things sacrificed to idols, we know, (for we all have knowledge: knowledge puffs up, but love edifies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If any one think he knows anything, he knows nothing yet as he ought to know [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if any one loveGod, he is known of him):

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — concerning then the eating of things sacrificed to idols, we know that an idol [is] nothing in [the] world, and that there [is] no otherGod save one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For and if indeed there are [those] calledgods, whether in heaven or on earth, (as there aregods many, and lords many,)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: yet to us [there is] oneGod, the Father, of whom all things, and we for him; and one Lord, Jesus Christ, by whom [are] all things, and we by him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But knowledge [is] not in all: but some, with conscience of the idol, until now eat as of a thing sacrificed to idols; and their conscience, being weak, is defiled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But meat does not commend us toGod; neither if we should not eat do we come short; nor if we should eat have we an advantage.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But see lest anywise this your right [to eat] itself be a stumbling-block to the weak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if any one see thee, who hast knowledge, sitting at table in an idol-house, shall not his conscience, he being weak, be emboldened to eat the things sacrificed to the idol?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the weak [one], the brother for whose sake Christ died, will perish through thy knowledge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now, thus sinning against the brethren, and wounding their weak conscience, ye sin against Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore if meat be a fall-trap to my brother, I will eat no flesh for ever, that I may not be a fall-trap to my brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Am I not free? am I not an apostle? have I not seen Jesus our Lord? are not ye my work in [the] Lord?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I am not an apostle to others, yet at any rate I am to you: for the seal of mine apostleship are ye in [the] Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My defence to those who examine me is this:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Have we not a right to eat and to drink?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: have we not a right to take round a sister [as] wife, as also the other apostles, and the brethren of the Lord, and Cephas?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Or I alone and Barnabas, have we not a right not to work?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who ever carries on war at his own charges? who plants a vineyard and does not eat of its fruit? or who herds a flock and does not eat of the milk of the flock?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do I speak these things as a man, or does not the law also say these things?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For in the law of Moses it is written, Thou shalt not muzzle the ox that is treading out corn. IsGod occupied about the oxen,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: or does he say [it] altogether for our sakes? For for our sakes it has been written, that the plougher should plough in hope, and he that treads out corn, in hope of partaking of [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If we have sown to you spiritual things, [is it a] great [thing] if we shall reap your carnal things?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If others partake of this right over you, should not rather we? But we have not used this right, but we bear all things, that we may put no hindrance in the way of the glad tidings of the Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do ye not know that they who labour [at] sacred things eat of the [offerings offered in the] temple; they that attend at the altar partake with the altar?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So also the Lord has ordained to those that announce the glad tidings to live of the glad tidings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I have used none of these things. Now I have not written these things that it should be thus in my case; for [it were] good for me rather to die than that any one should make vain my boast.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if I announce the glad tidings, I have nothing to boast of; for a necessity is laid upon me; for it is woe to me if I should not announce the glad tidings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if I do this voluntarily, I have a reward; but if not of my own will, I am entrusted with an administration.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What is the reward then that I have? That in announcing the glad tidings I make the glad tidings costless [to others], so as not to have made use, as belonging to me, of my right in [announcing] the glad tidings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For being free from all, I have made myself bondman to all, that I might gain the most [possible].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I became to the Jews as a Jew, in order that I might gain the Jews: to those under law, as under law, not being myself under law, in order that I might gain those under law:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to those without law, as without law, (not as without law toGod, but as legitimately subject to Christ,) in order that I might gain [those] without law.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I became to the weak, [as] weak, in order that I might gain the weak. To all I have become all things, in order that at all events I might save some.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I do all things for the sake of the glad tidings, that I may be fellow-partaker with them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Know ye not that they who run in [the] race-course run all, but one receives the prize? Thus run in order that ye may obtain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But every one that contends [for a prize] is temperate in all things: they then indeed that they may receive a corruptible crown, but we an incorruptible.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I therefore thus run, as not uncertainly; so I combat, as not beating the air.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I buffet my body, and lead it captive, lest [after] having preached to others I should be myself rejected.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I would not have you ignorant, brethren, that all our fathers were under the cloud, and all passed through the sea;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all were baptised unto Moses in the cloud and in the sea;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all ate the same spiritual food,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all drank the same spiritual drink, for they drank of a spiritual rock which followed [them]: (now the rock was the Christ;)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: yetGod was not pleased with the most of them, for they were strewed in the desert.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But these things happened [as] types of us, that we should not be lusters after evil things, as they also lusted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Neither be ye idolaters, as some of them; as it is written, The people sat down to eat and to drink, and rose up to play.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Neither let us commit fornication, as some of them committed fornication, and fell in one day three and twenty thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Neither let us tempt the Christ, as some of them tempted, and perished by serpents.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Neither murmur ye, as some of them murmured, and perished by the destroyer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now all these things happened to them [as] types, and have been written for our admonition, upon whom the ends of the ages are come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So that let him that thinks that he stands take heed lest he fall.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: No temptation has taken you but such as is according to man's nature; andGod is faithful, who will not suffer you to be tempted above what ye are able [to bear], but will with the temptation make the issue also, so that [ye] should be able to bear [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I speak as to intelligent [persons]: do ye judge what I say.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The cup of blessing which we bless, is it not [the] communion of the blood of the Christ? The bread which we break, is it not [the] communion of the body of the Christ?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because we, [being] many, are one loaf, one body; for we all partake of that one loaf.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: See Israel according to flesh: are not they who eat the sacrifices in communion with the altar?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What then do I say? that what is sacrificed to an idol is anything, or that an idol is anything?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But that what [the nations] sacrifice they sacrifice to demons, and not toGod. Now I do not wish you to be in communion with demons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye cannot drink [the] Lord's cup, and [the] cup of demons: ye cannot partake of [the] Lord's table, and of [the] table of demons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? are we stronger than he?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All things are lawful, but all are not profitable; all things are lawful, but all do not edify.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let no one seek his own [advantage], but that of the other.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Everything sold in the shambles eat, making no inquiry for conscience sake.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the earth [is] the Lord's and its fulness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if any one of the unbelievers invite you, and ye are minded to go, all that is set before you eat, making no inquiry for conscience sake.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if any one say to you, This is offered to holy purposes, do not eat, for his sake that pointed it out, and conscience sake;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but conscience, I mean, not thine own, but that of the other: for why is my liberty judged by another conscience?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I partake with thanksgiving, why am I spoken evil of for what I give thanks for?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whether therefore ye eat, or drink, or whatever ye do, do all things toGod's glory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give no occasion to stumbling, whether to Jews, or Greeks, or the assembly ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Even as I also please all in all things; not seeking my own profit, but that of the many, that they may be saved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be my imitators, even as I also [am] of Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now I praise you, that in all things ye are mindful of me; and that as I have directed you, ye keep the directions.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I wish you to know that the Christ is the head of every man, but woman's head [is] the man, and the Christ's headGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Every man praying or prophesying, having [anything] on his head, puts his head to shame.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But every woman praying or prophesying with her head uncovered puts her own head to shame; for it is one and the same as a shaved [woman].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if a woman be not covered, let her hair also be cut off. But if [it be] shameful to a woman to have her hair cut off or to be shaved, let her be covered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For man indeed ought not to have his head covered, beingGod's image and glory; but woman is man's glory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For man is not of woman, but woman of man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For also man was not created for the sake of the woman, but woman for the sake of the man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore ought the woman to have authority on her head, on account of the angels.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: However, neither [is] woman without man, nor man without woman, in [the] Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For as the woman [is] of the man, so also [is] the man by the woman, but all things ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Judge in yourselves: is it comely that a woman should pray toGod uncovered?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Does not even nature itself teach you, that man, if he have long hair, it is a dishonour to him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But woman, if she have long hair, [it is] glory to her; for the long hair is given [to her] in lieu of a veil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if any one think to be contentious, we have no such custom, nor the assemblies ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But [in] prescribing [to you on] this [which I now enter on], I do not praise, [namely,] that ye come together, not for the better, but for the worse.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For first, when ye come together in assembly, I hear there exist divisions among you, and I partly give credit [to it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For there must also be sects among you, that the approved may become manifest among you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When ye come therefore together into one place, it is not to eat [the] Lord's supper.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For each one in eating takes his own supper before [others], and one is hungry and another drinks to excess.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Have ye not then houses for eating and drinking? or do ye despise the assembly ofGod, and put to shame them who have not? What shall I say to you? shall I praise you? In this [point] I do not praise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I received from the Lord, that which I also delivered to you, that the Lord Jesus, in the night in which he was delivered up, took bread,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having given thanks broke [it], and said, This is my body, which [is] for you: this do in remembrance of me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In like manner also the cup, after having supped, saying, This cup is the new covenant in my blood: this do, as often as ye shall drink [it], in remembrance of me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For as often as ye shall eat this bread, and drink the cup, ye announce the death of the Lord, until he come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So that whosoever shall eat the bread, or drink the cup of the Lord, unworthily, shall be guilty in respect of the body and of the blood of the Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But let a man prove himself, and thus eat of the bread, and drink of the cup.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For [the] eater and drinker eats and drinks judgment to himself, not distinguishing the body.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On this account many among you [are] weak and infirm, and a good many are fallen asleep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if we judged ourselves, so were we not judged.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But being judged, we are disciplined of [the] Lord, that we may not be condemned with the world.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So that, my brethren, when ye come together to eat, wait for one another.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If any one be hungry, let him eat at home, that ye may not come together for judgment. But the other things, whenever I come, I will set in order.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But concerning spiritual [manifestations], brethren, I do not wish you to be ignorant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye know that when ye were [of the] nations [ye were] led away to dumb idols, in whatever way ye might be led.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I give you therefore to know, that no one, speaking in [the power of the] Spirit ofGod, says, Curse [on] Jesus; and no one can say, Lord Jesus, unless in [the power of the] Holy Spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But there are distinctions of gifts, but the same Spirit;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and there are distinctions of services, and the same Lord;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and there are distinctions of operations, but the sameGod who operates all things in all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But to each the manifestation of the Spirit is given for profit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For to one, by the Spirit, is given [the] word of wisdom; and to another [the] word of knowledge, according to the same Spirit;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and to a different one faith, in [the power of] the same Spirit; and to another gifts of healing in [the power of] the same Spirit;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and to another operations of miracles; and to another prophecy; and to another discerning of spirits; and to a different one kinds of tongues; and to another interpretation of tongues.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But all these things operates the one and the same Spirit, dividing to each in particular according as he pleases.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For even as the body is one and has many members, but all the members of the body, being many, are one body, so also [is] the Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For also in [the power of] one Spirit we have all been baptised into one body, whether Jews or Greeks, whether bondmen or free, and have all been given to drink of one Spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For also the body is not one member but many.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If the foot say, Because I am not a hand I am not of the body, is it on account of this not indeed of the body?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if the ear say, Because I am not an eye I am not of the body, is it on account of this not indeed of the body?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If the whole body [were] an eye, where the hearing? if all hearing, where the smelling?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But nowGod has set the members, each one of them in the body, according as it has pleased [him].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if all were one member, where the body?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But now the members [are] many, and the body one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The eye cannot say to the hand, I have not need of thee; or again, the head to the feet, I have not need of you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But much rather, the members of the body which seem to be weaker are necessary;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and those [parts] of the body which we esteem to be the more void of honour, these we clothe with more abundant honour; and our uncomely [parts] have more abundant comeliness;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but our comely [parts] have not need. ButGod has tempered the body together, having given more abundant honour to [the part] that lacked;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that there might be no division in the body, but that the members might have the same concern one for another.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if one member suffer, all the members suffer with [it]; and if one member be glorified, all the members rejoice with [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now ye are Christ's body, and members in particular.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod has set certain in the assembly: first, apostles; secondly, prophets; thirdly, teachers; then miraculous powers; then gifts of healings; helps; governments; kinds of tongues.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Are] all apostles? [are] all prophets? [are] all teachers? [are] all [in possession of] miraculous powers?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: have all gifts of healings? do all speak with tongues? do all interpret?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But desire earnestly the greater gifts, and yet shew I unto you a way of more surpassing excellence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I speak with the tongues of men and of angels, but have not love, I am become sounding brass or a clanging cymbal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if I have prophecy, and know all mysteries and all knowledge, and if I have all faith, so as to remove mountains, but have not love, I am nothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if I shall dole out all my goods in food, and if I deliver up my body that I may be burned, but have not love, I profit nothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Love has long patience, is kind; love is not emulous [of others]; love is not insolent and rash, is not puffed up,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: does not behave in an unseemly manner, does not seek what is its own, is not quickly provoked, does not impute evil,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: does not rejoice at iniquity but rejoices with the truth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Love never fails; but whether prophecies, they shall be done away; or tongues, they shall cease; or knowledge, it shall be done away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we know in part, and we prophesy in part:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but when that which is perfect has come, that which is in part shall be done away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When I was a child, I spoke as a child, I felt as a child, I reasoned as a child; when I became a man, I had done with what belonged to the child.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we see now through a dim window obscurely, but then face to face; now I know partially, but then I shall know according as I also have been known.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now abide faith, hope, love; these three things; and the greater of these [is] love.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Follow after love, and be emulous of spiritual [manifestations], but rather that ye may prophesy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he that speaks with a tongue does not speak to men but toGod: for no one hears; but in spirit he speaks mysteries.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he that prophesies speaks to men [in] edification, and encouragement, and consolation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that speaks with a tongue edifies himself; but he that prophesies edifies [the] assembly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now I desire that ye should all speak with tongues, but rather that ye should prophesy. But greater is he that prophesies than he that speaks with tongues, unless he interpret, that the assembly may receive edification.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, brethren, if I come to you speaking with tongues, what shall I profit you, unless I shall speak to you either in revelation, or in knowledge, or in prophecy, or in teaching?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Even lifeless things giving a sound, whether pipe or harp, if they give not distinction to the sounds, how shall it be known what is piped or harped?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For also, if the trumpet give an uncertain sound, who shall prepare himself for war?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus also ye with the tongue, unless ye give a distinct speech, how shall it be known what is spoken? for ye will be speaking to the air.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There are, it may be, so many kinds of voices in the world, and none of undistinguishable sound.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If therefore I do not know the power of the sound, I shall be to him that speaks a barbarian, and he that speaks a barbarian for me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus ye also, since ye are desirous of spirits, seek that ye may abound for the edification of the assembly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore let him that speaks with a tongue pray that he may interpret.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if I pray with a tongue, my spirit prays, but my understanding is unfruitful.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What is it then? I will pray with the spirit, but I will pray also with the understanding; I will sing with the spirit, but I will sing also with the understanding.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Since otherwise, if thou blessest with [the] spirit, how shall he who fills the place of the simple [Christian] say Amen, at thy giving of thanks, since he does not know what thou sayest?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou indeed givest thanks well, but the other is not edified.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I thankGod I speak in a tongue more than all of you:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but in [the] assembly I desire to speak five words with my understanding, that I may instruct others also, [rather] than ten thousand words in a tongue.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Brethren, be not children in [your] minds, but in malice be babes; but in [your] minds be grown [men].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is written in the law, By people of other tongues, and by strange lips, will I speak to this people; and neither thus will they hear me, saith the Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So that tongues are for a sign, not to those who believe, but to unbelievers; but prophecy, not to unbelievers, but to those who believe.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If therefore the whole assembly come together in one place, and all speak with tongues, and simple [persons] enter in, or unbelievers, will not they say ye are mad?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if all prophesy, and some unbeliever or simple [person] come in, he is convicted of all, he is judged of all;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the secrets of his heart are manifested; and thus, falling upon [his] face, he will do homage toGod, reporting thatGod is indeed amongst you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What is it then, brethren? whenever ye come together, each [of you] has a psalm, has a teaching, has a tongue, has a revelation, has an interpretation. Let all things be done to edification.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If any one speak with a tongue, [let it be] two, or at the most three, and separately, and let one interpret;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but if there be no interpreter, let him be silent in [the] assembly, and let him speak to himself and toGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And let two or three prophets speak, and let the others judge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if there be a revelation to another sitting [there], let the first be silent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For ye can all prophesy one by one, that all may learn and all be encouraged.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And spirits of prophets are subject to prophets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ForGod is not [aGod] of disorder but of peace, as in all the assemblies of the saints.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let [your] women be silent in the assemblies, for it is not permitted to them to speak; but to be in subjection, as the law also says.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if they wish to learn anything, let them ask their own husbands at home; for it is a shame for a woman to speak in assembly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Did the word ofGod go out from you, or did it come to you only?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If any one thinks himself to be a prophet or spiritual, let him recognise the things that I write to you, that it is [the] Lord's commandment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if any be ignorant, let him be ignorant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So that, brethren, desire to prophesy, and do not forbid the speaking with tongues.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But let all things be done comelily and with order.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I make known to you, brethren, the glad tidings which I announced to you, which also ye received, in which also ye stand,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: by which also ye are saved, (if ye hold fast the word which I announced to you as the glad tidings,) unless indeed ye have believed in vain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I delivered to you, in the first place, what also I had received, that Christ died for our sins, according to the scriptures;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and that he was buried; and that he was raised the third day, according to the scriptures;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and that he appeared to Cephas, then to the twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he appeared to above five hundred brethren at once, of whom the most remain until now, but some also have fallen asleep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he appeared to James; then to all the apostles;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and last of all, as to an abortion, he appeared to me also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I am the least of the apostles, who am not fit to be called apostle, because I have persecuted the assembly ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But byGod's grace I am what I am; and his grace, which [was] towards me, has not been vain; but I have laboured more abundantly than they all, but not I, but the grace ofGod which [was] with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whether, therefore, I or they, thus we preach, and thus ye have believed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now if Christ is preached that he is raised from among [the] dead, how say some among you that there is not a resurrection of [those that are] dead?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if there is not a resurrection of [those that are] dead, neither is Christ raised:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but if Christ is not raised, then, indeed, vain also [is] our preaching, and vain also your faith.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we are found also false witnesses ofGod; for we have witnessed concerningGod that he raised the Christ, whom he has not raised if indeed [those that are] dead are not raised.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if [those that are] dead are not raised, neither is Christ raised;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but if Christ be not raised, your faith [is] vain; ye are yet in your sins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then indeed also those who have fallen asleep in Christ have perished.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If in this life only we have hope in Christ, we are [the] most miserable of all men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (But now Christ is raised from among [the] dead, first-fruits of those fallen asleep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For since by man [came] death, by man also resurrection of [those that are] dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For as in the Adam all die, thus also in the Christ all shall be made alive.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But each in his own rank: [the] first-fruits, Christ; then those that are the Christ's at his coming.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the end, when he gives up the kingdom to him [who is]God and Father; when he shall have annulled all rule and all authority and power.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he must reign until he put all enemies under his feet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [The] last enemy [that] is annulled [is] death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he has put all things in subjection under his feet. But when he says that all things are put in subjection, [it is] evident that [it is] except him who put all things in subjection to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when all things shall have been brought into subjection to him, then the Son also himself shall be placed in subjection to him who put all things in subjection to him, thatGod may be all in all.)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Since what shall the baptised for the dead do if [those that are] dead rise not at all? why also are they baptised for them?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why do we also endanger ourselves every hour?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Daily I die, by your boasting which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If, [to speak] after the manner of man, I have fought with beasts in Ephesus, what is the profit to me if [those that are] dead do not rise? let us eat and drink; for to-morrow we die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be not deceived: evil communications corrupt good manners.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Awake up righteously, and sin not; for some are ignorant ofGod: I speak to you as a matter of shame.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But some one will say, How are the dead raised? and with what body do they come?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Fool; what thou sowest is not quickened unless it die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And what thou sowest, thou sowest not the body that shall be, but a bare grain: it may be of wheat, or some one of the rest:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: andGod gives to it a body as he has pleased, and to each of the seeds its own body.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Every flesh [is] not the same flesh, but one [is] of men, and another flesh of beasts, and another [flesh] of birds, and another of fishes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [there are] heavenly bodies, and earthly bodies: but different is the glory of the heavenly, different that of the earthly:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: one [the] sun's glory, and another [the] moon's glory, and another [the] stars' glory; for star differs from star in glory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus also [is] the resurrection of the dead. It is sown in corruption, it is raised in incorruptibility.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is sown in dishonour, it is raised in glory. It is sown in weakness, it is raised in power.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is sown a natural body, it is raised a spiritual body: if there is a natural body, there is also a spiritual [one].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus also it is written, The first man Adam became a living soul; the last Adam a quickening spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But that which is spiritual [was] not first, but that which is natural, then that which is spiritual:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the first man out of [the] earth, made of dust; the second man, out of heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Such as he made of dust, such also those made of dust; and such as the heavenly [one], such also the heavenly [ones].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as we have borne the image of the [one] made of dust, we shall bear also the image of the heavenly [one].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But this I say, brethren, that flesh and blood cannot inheritGod's kingdom, nor does corruption inherit incorruptibility.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I tell you a mystery: We shall not all fall asleep, but we shall all be changed,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in an instant, in [the] twinkling of an eye, at the last trumpet; for the trumpet shall sound, and the dead shall be raised incorruptible, and we shall be changed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this corruptible must needs put on incorruptibility, and this mortal put on immortality.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when this corruptible shall have put on incorruptibility, and this mortal shall have put on immortality, then shall come to pass the word written: Death has been swallowed up in victory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Where, O death, [is] thy sting? where, O death, thy victory?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now the sting of death [is] sin, and the power of sin the law;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but thanks toGod, who gives us the victory by our Lord Jesus Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So then, my beloved brethren, be firm, immovable, abounding always in the work of the Lord, knowing that your toil is not in vain in [the] Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now concerning the collection for the saints, as I directed the assemblies of Galatia, so do ye do also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On [the] first of [the] week let each of you put by at home, laying up [in] whatever [degree] he may have prospered, that there may be no collections when I come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when I am arrived, whomsoever ye shall approve, these I will send with letters to carry your bounty to Jerusalem:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and if it be suitable that I also should go, they shall go with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I will come to you when I shall have gone through Macedonia; for I do go through Macedonia.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But perhaps I will stay with you, or even winter with you, that ye may set me forward wheresoever I may go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I will not see you now in passing, for I hope to remain a certain time with you, if the Lord permit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I remain in Ephesus until Pentecost.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For a great door is opened to me and an effectual [one], and [the] adversaries many.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now if Timotheus come, see that he may be with you without fear; for he works the work of the Lord, even as I.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let not therefore any one despise him; but set him forward in peace, that he may come to me; for I expect him with the brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now concerning the brother Apollos, I begged him much that he would go to you with the brethren; but it was not at all [his] will to go now; but he will come when he shall have good opportunity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be vigilant; stand fast in the faith; quit yourselves like men; be strong.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let all things ye do be done in love.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I beseech you, brethren, (ye know the house of Stephanas, that it is the first-fruits of Achaia, and they have devoted themselves to the saints for service,)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that ye should also be subject to such, and to every one joined in the work and labouring.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I rejoice in the coming of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus; because they have supplied what was lacking on your part.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they have refreshed my spirit and yours: own therefore such.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The assemblies of Asia salute you. Aquila and Priscilla, with the assembly in their house, salute you much in [the] Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the brethren salute you. Salute one another with a holy kiss.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The salutation of [me] Paul with my own hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If any one love not the Lord [Jesus Christ] let him be Anathema Maranatha.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My love [be] with you all in Christ Jesus. Amen.
